title: 10 possible side effects of Barosma betulina (Buchu) if used improperly
intro: Barosma betulina, also known as Buchu, is a plant commonly used in traditional medicine, but if used improperly, it can cause several health issues. Overuse or improper use of Buchu can lead to stomach irritation, nausea, and diarrhea. In severe cases, it can cause kidney damage due to its diuretic properties. Pregnant women should avoid Buchu as it may stimulate menstruation and lead to miscarriage. Additionally, Buchu may interact with certain medications, particularly blood thinners, and can increase the risk of bleeding. Therefore, it is crucial to consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new supplement regimen, including the use of Buchu.
